candidates.title: Western Manitoba First Nation declares state of emergency as flooding sparks calls for federal aid
candidates.description: Minegoziibe Anishinabe First Nation, in Manitoba’s flood-stricken Parkland area, has declared a state of emergency due to washed out infrastructure, joining more than 30 communities that have done the same.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario pushed ahead with strong mayor powers expansion despite 'predominantly negative' feedback
candidates.description: Premier Doug Ford’s sweeping expansion of strong mayor powers to nearly half of Ontario’s municipalities was met with "predominantly negative" feedback during consultations, with respondents calling the system “unnecessary” and “undemocratic," documents obtained by CBC News reveal.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Want to cool off with a dip in a river or lake? Here's how to stay safe
candidates.description: After a rash of drowning deaths during Europe's ongoing record-breaking heat wave, advocates are urging Canadians to stay safe this Canada Day as they push for more public swimming access across the country.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Search ongoing for 7-year-old boy who went missing at Hamilton conservation area
candidates.description: Hamilton police are searching the Binbrook Conservation Area for a seven-year-old boy who was last seen on the beach, by the floating inflatable playground.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Quesnel, B.C., rolling out the red carpet to attract U.S.-trained doctors — and it's working
candidates.description: The community of Quesnel in northern B.C. is pulling out all the stops to attract U.S.-trained physicians and solve previously long waits for family doctors. A health-care recruiter in the community of around 10,000 people says the so-called "red carpet" approach is working — with at least four doctors committed to work there by the end of the summer.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Quebec's biggest landfill gets emergency approval to pile garbage higher
candidates.description: The Quebec government has authorized the temporary expansion of the province's largest landfill north of Montreal. The Terrebonne site is expected to reach its maximum capacity in less than a year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Prince Edward Islanders to get extra money from province to help with rising costs
candidates.description: Some Prince Edward Islanders will see extra money in their bank accounts on Friday as the province rolls out its new Island Essentials Benefit.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Evacuation order to be reassessed as rainfall helped fight Labrador West wildfires, mayor says
candidates.description: Rainfall over wildfires burning near Labrador West provided some much needed relief on Wednesday, but the mayor of Labrador City says it's still too early to know when an evacuation order could be lifted for residents.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario farmers embrace extreme heat after weeks of storms, hail damage crops
candidates.description: Farmers in southwestern Ontario say that weeks of extreme weather events, including torrential rain and hail, has made growing crops a challenge. Some say they're embracing a week of hot temperatures, which can help crops get healthy.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ottawa thunderstorms keep Carney from delivering Canada Day unity speech in Edmonton
candidates.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney urged Canadians to remain united in a Canada Day speech in the nation's capital on Wednesday, but his plan to deliver similar remarks in Edmonton later in the day was upended by thunderstorms that prevented his plane from taking off on time.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'Extreme weather' wipes out Ottawa's Canada Day activities, including fireworks
candidates.description: Extreme weather on Wednesday caused the cancellation of Ottawa's evening Canada Day activities, including the fireworks display, as homes flooded, roads were closed and thunderstorms led to power outages.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.L. government declares fire ban for all Labrador, until at least July 13
candidates.description: The Minister of Forestry has ordered a blanket fire ban for Labrador, effective to at least July 13.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Albertans express patriotism, melancholy on Canada Day amid talk of separation
candidates.description: The possibility of Alberta separating from Canada was top of mind for Maple Leaf-clad Albertans in the province's largest city as they celebrated the birth of Confederation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Teen driver charged after man on riding lawnmower killed in fatal collision in North Dumfries, Ont.
candidates.description: A 16-year-old girl has been charged with dangerous operation of a vehicle causing death, speeding and stunt driving after a collision between a car and riding lawnmower last month in southern Ontario. A 51-year-old North Dumfries Township man died.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why one superfan thinks now is the right time for Canada's Eurovision debut
candidates.description: When it was announced that Canada would be entering the 2027 Eurovision Song Contest — it was music to Matti McLean’s ears. A Canadian superfan of the international music competition, co-host of the Think About Eurovision podcast and creator of the Fringe Festival show A Canadian Explains Eurovision to Other Canadians, McLean says he has been campaigning for years to see this moment become a reality.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canadians mark Canada Day as Carney urges unity, heat grips Central Canada
candidates.description: Officials in many provinces are urging people to stay hydrated and check on vulnerable neighbours as communities kick off celebrations for Canada's 159th birthday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada will enter the 2027 Eurovision Song Contest
candidates.description: CBC/Radio-Canada and the European Broadcasting Union have announced Canada will participate in the 2027 Eurovision Song Contest in Bulgaria.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Application portal opens for $100 affordability payments for Albertans
candidates.description: Albertans can start applying today for $100 that the provincial government says is intended to help deal with the high cost of living.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump isn't extending CUSMA trade deal, so what happens now?
candidates.description: The Trump administration surprised no one with its long-expected announcement Wednesday that the U.S. would not join Canada and Mexico in extending the free trade deal between the three countries. Where things go from here, however, is anyone's guess.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Deadly, large-scale Russian attack shakes Kyiv as Ukrainian strikes batter Moscow's oil sector
candidates.description: Russia hammered Kyiv with an 11-hour drone and missile attack overnight into Thursday morning, killing at least 21 civilians in the city and injuring scores more, in what Moscow said was retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil facilities.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran warns oil tankers to use approved routes in Strait of Hormuz or face 'forceful response'
candidates.description: Iran's joint military command warned Thursday that all oil tankers moving through the Strait of Hormuz must use its approved routes or face a "forceful response," again ratcheting up tensions over a waterway crucial for international energy supplies.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 9 monks struck and killed in Thailand by 11-year-old driver
candidates.description: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck into a group of monks on a pilgrimage walk in northeastern Thailand on Thursday, killing nine, officials said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump won't return U.S. to full war in Iran 'unless he has to,' Vance says
candidates.description: ‌Iran and the United States concluded a round of indirect talks Wednesday with no sign they had made headway toward a lasting peace, focusing instead on issues that they had supposedly resolved two weeks ago.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Officials fear medical crisis as Venezuelans displaced by quakes crowd shelters, hospitals
candidates.description: Thousands of displaced Venezuelans are living in dismal sanitary conditions without access to clean water as already strained hospitals struggle to cope with an influx of emergency patients following the June 24 earthquakes that have killed more than 2,000. Aid workers say the country is facing a major medical crisis that, unless quickly controlled, will take more lives in the days and weeks ahead.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Soccer fans file class-action lawsuit against StubHub over cancelled World Cup tickets
candidates.description: Soccer fans in the U.S. are suing StubHub, accusing the resale platform of failing to deliver expensive tickets for the FIFA World Cup that they bought on the secondary market.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. declines to extend CUSMA trade deal with Canada, Mexico
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration officially announced that it's opting not to extend its trade agreement with Canada and Mexico, triggering what are expected to be tough negotiations on amending the deal.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 2 people arrested after hanging 'power of love' banner on Empire State Building antenna
candidates.description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building's antenna and unfurled a banner about "the power of love" at midday Tuesday, before starting to descend, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's crypto businesses took in about $1.2B US last year, federal filing shows
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump took in nearly $1.2 billion US from his crypto businesses last year, a federal filing released Tuesday shows, locking in profits as his investors suffered losses.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals he has Alzheimer's disease
candidates.description: Actor and activist Danny Glover, best known for starring as an easy-going police officer in the Lethal Weapon franchise, has revealed he has Alzheimer's disease.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How extreme heat is changing Europe
candidates.description: Cities across Europe have hit record temperatures during this year’s heatwave. As extreme heat becomes more common, what does it mean for life across the continent?
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: The Vespa at 80: Why the Italian scooter remains the coolest thing on 2 wheels
candidates.description: Thousands of Vespa riders have been moving in swarms through Rome to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the iconic Italian scooter, which became famous in the 1953 film Roman Holiday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Republicans portray Canadian border as a threat, but U.S. statistics tell a different story
candidates.description: Republican members of Congress tried turning up the heat on Canada on Tuesday, depicting the northern border as a dangerous security threat allowing lots of drugs and illegal migrants into the U.S.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Shipping costs soar as retailers try to secure orders ahead of fresh tariffs
candidates.description: Retailers are rushing to import products ahead of possible new tariffs expected to land at the end of July. That's pushing the cost to move products by boat way up — and industry experts say consumers will bear the brunt of that extra cost.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta government to announce details for West Coast pipeline proposal
candidates.description: The Alberta government is expected to announce details on Thursday regarding its proposal to build a new oil pipeline stretching to the West Coast.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Regulator launches inquiry into contentious fees charged by Rogers, Bell and Telus
candidates.description: The Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission has ordered Canada's three largest telecoms to justify their contentious fees and explain why they shouldn't face fines for apparent federal violations.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
candidates.description: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park, and encouraged them as they inched closer to him.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 22
candidates.description: The latest barrage deployed the largest number of weapons on the capital and hit locations over a wide area.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
candidates.description: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
candidates.description: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Turkish police beat us with iron rods before we lost limbs to frostbite, Afghans say
candidates.description: The young migrants heading to Europe say they were stripped and had their hands tied in freezing temperatures.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Four die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
candidates.description: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
candidates.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation
candidates.description: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
candidates.description: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Banned by Beijing, this comedian is taking his act to Chinese speakers abroad
candidates.description: Chizi, one of China’s biggest stand-up comedians, has found a new stage after falling afoul of China's censors.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canada celebrates its birthday as Mark Carney battles to keep it intact
candidates.description: As Canadians celebrate their nation's birthday, independence movements in two provinces are eyeing the exit from the country.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
candidates.description: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
candidates.description: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Beautiful and terrible' - the world's thoughts on the US at 250
candidates.description: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Americans react to Supreme Court upholding birthright citizenship
candidates.description: The BBC asked Americans how they felt after the Supreme Court's ruling on citizenship for babies born in the US.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Meta glasses wearers hit with paywall to use built-in feature
candidates.description: A feature in Meta's glasses which boosts the voice of people will be capped at three hours for free use.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Google must pay €4.1bn fine for using Android to 'block' rivals
candidates.description: A Google spokesperson said the judgement "fails to recognise" the firm's "significant investment to ensure Android remains open."
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: PlayStation will stop releasing games on discs in 2028
candidates.description: The firm said future games would still be sold in shops, but they would come with a digital code only.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: England's warmest June on record following historic heatwave
candidates.description: England saw its warmest June since records began in 1865 and the second warmest for the whole of the UK according to data just released by the Met Office.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Farming worries after India records driest June in over a decade
candidates.description: Weaker monsoon rains are likely to impact farm output but the government says it has enough grain stocks to deal with any shortages.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Three beaver kits born in Wyre Forest enclosure
candidates.description: New beaver kits are captured on camera after a family of six beavers were first released in 2024.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
candidates.description: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
candidates.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Vatican declares Society of St. Pius X in schism, excommunicates bishops
candidates.description: The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Russia hits Ukraine's capital with a massive drone and missile attack, killing at least 18
candidates.description: Russia launched a large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital overnight into Thursday, with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones, killing several people.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran hold separate meetings in Qatar and agree to continue discussions
candidates.description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A major Russian attack kills 17 in Kyiv as Ukraine keeps striking Moscow's oil sector
candidates.description: Loud explosions shook Kyiv for hours during the night, with many people sheltering at subway stations. Emergency crews were digging through the rubble of collapsed buildings in search of victims.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Untold casualties and humanitarian needs: What to know a week from Venezuela's quakes
candidates.description: Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Funerals held for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring center collapse
candidates.description: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
candidates.description: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought, beats Ecuador to advance in World Cup
candidates.description: Mexico lost seven consecutive times at that same stage from 1994 to 2018 and didn't advance past the group stage in 2022. Now, the 40-year wait is over.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. job market slows in June
candidates.description: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, the Labor Department said on Friday, as jobs growth slowed from the previous two months, while the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2%.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Meta considered buying Kalshi before developing its own prediction market app
candidates.description: Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe
candidates.description: Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
candidates.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse
candidates.description: Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: As a heatwave grips the U.S., a look at how extreme heat affects the body
candidates.description: As heatwaves sweep the country, NPR's A Martinez asks W. Larry Kenney, professor of physiology and kinesiology at Penn State University, how extreme heat affects humans.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
candidates.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 5 safety tips to keep you out of the emergency room this summer
candidates.description: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
candidates.description: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
candidates.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA scientists flag concerns with peptides, the trendy molecules RFK Jr. supports
candidates.description: Documents reviewing the research on peptides note lack of good evidence of efficacy or safety. Yet an FDA panel will consider easing access to them later this month.
candidates.author: NPR
